# Tellie for Developers

> CLI, MCP server, and the tellie:// URL scheme for Tellie, the silent second
> screen that lives in the Mac notch. Anything that can run a command can push a
> glanceable status to your notch, from your terminal, a script, CI, or an AI
> agent, and read it back.

The full, always-current agent manual is hosted at https://tellieapp.com/llms.txt
(point a capable agent there and say "Tellie me").

## Start here

- [README](https://github.com/ChazinMac/tellie-dev/blob/main/README.md)
- [Cookbook](https://github.com/ChazinMac/tellie-dev/tree/main/cookbook) — recipes for the notch surface
- [Terminal cheatsheet](https://github.com/ChazinMac/tellie-dev/blob/main/CHEATSHEET.md) — every verb and flag
- [Full developer docs](https://tellieapp.com/developers)

## Packages

- `@tellie/cli` — the `tellie` command-line tool (`update` / `flash` / `send` / `clear` / `status` / `log`, the Claude Code hook, the shared fleet feed)
- `@tellie/mcp` — an MCP server so MCP-aware AI clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) can push to the notch

## The story

Tellie was built in about 3 days by a 30-year Apple veteran pairing with AI. The
origin story: https://stevechazin.com/i-built-a-mac-app-in-3-days-and-you-can-too/
